  "findings": [
    "Wrong oil type installed in A/C compressor — oil specification does not match compressor type per OEM requirements",
    "Non-compliant lubricant creates risk of inadequate lubrication, accelerated wear, and potential compressor seizure",
    "System is operating with incorrect refrigerant oil chemistry, risking chemical incompatibility with seals and desiccant"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Shut down A/C system to prevent further compressor damage",
    "Recover refrigerant per EPA 609 procedures",
    "Flush system to remove incorrect oil and contaminants",
    "Recharge with OEM-specified oil type and correct refrigerant quantity",
    "Verify compressor operation and leak-check after service"
  ],
  "rationale": "Using the wrong oil for the compressor type is a non-compliant condition that violates OEM specifications. Different compressor designs (e.g., PAG vs. POE vs. mineral oil) require specific oil chemistries for proper lubrication and refrigerant compatibility. Operating with incorrect oil causes inadequate lubrication, accelerated wear, and can lead to compressor failure. EPA 609 governs proper refrigerant recovery during corrective service. Since the system is in-service with a non-compliant lubricant, the condition is a fail with high risk of imminent component degradation or failure.",
  "references": ["EPA 609", "OEM specs"]
}
